jokes.text: Oh,why,is there a place closer that sells them?
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Dwight went all the way to NYC for bagels
jokes.punchline: Dwight's deadpan response implying nowhere else sells bagels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - his literal interpretation and superiority complex about bagel knowledge delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Actually,i had two fish stick sandwiches. My girlfriend didn't want hers, because i guess i'm the only aphrodisiac she needs.
jokes.setup: Michael casually mentioning breakfast
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his sex life with inappropriate confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael cringe - completely inappropriate oversharing delivered with oblivious pride

jokes.text: Fish sticks are not an aaphrodisiac. You're thinking of deer penis.
jokes.setup: Michael's fish stick aphrodisiac comment
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact correction with bizarre alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment - his bizarre knowledge delivered with complete authority, perfectly timed correction that's somehow even weirder than Michael's comment

jokes.text: Can't a guy just buy some bagels for his friends so they'll owe him a favor, which he can use to get someone fired who stole a co-manager position from him anymore?
jokes.setup: Dwight's seemingly innocent bagel gesture
jokes.punchline: Reveals his manipulative scheme mid-sentence with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - starts as innocent question, escalates to reveal Dwight's Machiavellian plotting, delivered with bewildered innocence

jokes.text: Jeez... When did everyone get so cynical?
jokes.setup: Dwight just revealed his manipulative bagel scheme
jokes.punchline: Immediately complains about cynicism after exposing his own manipulation
jokes.explanation: Beautiful ironic follow-up - Dwight's complete lack of self-awareness after revealing his scheme

jokes.text: Oh,that's right,you're a woman,and you need to refuse food the first time. I'll try again. Please,pam,reconsider and have a bagel.
jokes.setup: Pam politely refuses a bagel
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre gender stereotyping and formal re-offering
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight misunderstanding of social norms, treating politeness like a formal protocol

jokes.text: I've always found beauty in uncommon places. Homeless people.
jokes.setup: Ryan describing his artistic photography
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning homeless people as subjects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan pretentiousness - exploitative art disguised as profound

jokes.text: Graffiti photography lets me capture all that.
jokes.setup: Ryan discussing his artistic photography
jokes.punchline: The pretentious way he describes photographing graffiti
jokes.explanation: Ryan's characteristic pretentiousness - making simple street photography sound profound

jokes.text: Birthday lunch.Birthday lunch-- there's no better medicine than birthday lunch.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing birthday lunch plans
jokes.punchline: Treating birthday lunch as literal medicine with repetitive enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Michael's childlike enthusiasm and literal interpretation of metaphors

jokes.text: so open wide,pam,and take a big ol' spoonful of birthday lunch medicine. Take with food.
jokes.setup: Michael's birthday lunch as medicine metaphor
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor with medicine instructions that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Michael extending his metaphor beyond logic, 'take with food' is absurdly redundant for lunch

jokes.text: Yeah,the rest of the story has been censored due to inappropriosity.
jokes.setup: Helene sharing intimate morning story
jokes.punchline: Michael's made-up word 'inappropriosity' while oversharing
jokes.explanation: Michael's characteristic word invention while inappropriately referencing sex at work

jokes.text: Hey,please,kevin... You're fired.
jokes.setup: Kevin asking inappropriate follow-up question
jokes.punchline: Michael's casual firing threat delivered as normal conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - starts as polite correction, escalates to firing with casual tone

jokes.text: Rarely,if ever.
jokes.setup: Pam saying 'isn't that always how it goes' about timing
jokes.punchline: Jim's literal correction of the cliché with perfect deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - puncturing cliché with literal accuracy, perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: Why did i get in the car? I could have struggled. I have a whistle in my purse.I didn't even blow it.
jokes.setup: Pam stuck at uncomfortable lunch
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mild regret to imagining dramatic escape tactics
jokes.explanation: Pam's escalating desperation, from simple regret to imagining kidnapping-level escape plans

jokes.text: Feel it against your cheek.
jokes.setup: Andy polishing Dwight's briefcase as a favor
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate intimate invitation regarding a briefcase
jokes.explanation: Andy's over-the-top approach to favor-giving, making briefcase polishing weirdly sensual

jokes.text: Do not test my politeness.
jokes.setup: Andy's excessive favor-giving to Dwight
jokes.punchline: Threatening tone about being too polite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - turning courtesy into aggressive competition, threatening with kindness

jokes.text: If you clench your buttocks together while walking, you can really take the pressure off your knees. Observe.
jokes.setup: Michael offering health advice at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate physical demonstration of butt-clenching in public
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate oversharing escalated to physical demonstration in restaurant

jokes.text: Do not walk around with your jacket cattywampus.
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his advice-giving
jokes.punchline: Using the silly word 'cattywampus' with serious authority
jokes.explanation: Michael using outdated slang with complete seriousness while micromanaging appearance

jokes.text: Oh,maybe michael will start dating that too.
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning childhood memories at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Bitter joke about Michael's dating patterns
jokes.explanation: Sharp, bitter humor from Pam - rare edge that shows her frustration with the situation

jokes.text: Ooh,burn.Burn on you. And a little bit on me too.
jokes.setup: Michael pointing out Pam's pregnancy math in front of her mother
jokes.punchline: Celebrating his own inappropriate comment then realizing it affects him
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish celebration of his burn followed by dawning awareness he insulted himself too

jokes.text: If anything,i am robbing the grave.
jokes.setup: Michael defending dating an older woman
jokes.punchline: Horrifically inappropriate metaphor about grave robbing
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark metaphor delivered with Michael's oblivious confidence - perfectly horrible and quotable

jokes.text: Grandma.
jokes.setup: Helene asking what grandma name he prefers
jokes.punchline: Michael's uncomfortable realization about their relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: The awkward pause and realization that he'd be dating a grandmother - visual comedy through discomfort

jokes.text: Well,there's no jumping in a triathlon. You're thinking the broad jump.
jokes.setup: Helene mentioning running and jumping in context of triathlon
jokes.punchline: Michael's pedantic correction about triathlon events
jokes.explanation: Michael's need to be technically correct even during relationship tension

jokes.text: But i-- i would like to if i wanted to.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he doesn't want to snowboard
jokes.punchline: Completely circular and meaningless justification
jokes.explanation: Michael's defensive circular logic that says absolutely nothing while trying to save face

jokes.text: Do i really want to go snowboarding? No. But i-- i would like to if i wanted to.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning his desires
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about wanting to want things
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic showing Michael's confused philosophy

jokes.text: There is no book.There's only a survival guide.
jokes.setup: Andy mentioning 'schrute book of niceness'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's serious correction about his life philosophy
jokes.explanation: Dwight treating life philosophy with military seriousness, correcting the metaphor

jokes.text: gosh,these tacos are awfully complicated to make. I will make everyone's tacos.
jokes.setup: Andy bringing lunch for everyone
jokes.punchline: Dwight's competitive overcomplication
jokes.explanation: Dwight's need to one-up Andy through taco complexity

jokes.text: I literally--i put no thought into the wrapping at all. Might as well have been toilet paper.
jokes.setup: Helene complimenting gift wrapping
jokes.punchline: Michael deflecting compliment awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Michael's self-deprecation during gift giving

jokes.text: "a scrapbook of our first memories,"by michael gary scott. For my girlfriend helene on your birthday."
jokes.setup: Michael's handmade birthday gift
jokes.punchline: Overly formal and awkward title page
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkward formality and overthinking of a simple gift title

jokes.text: Well,that's because you and i have very different taste. And you like lame things.
jokes.setup: Helene complimenting his scrapbook gift
jokes.punchline: Michael insulting her taste while she's being nice about his gift
jokes.explanation: Michael's self-sabotaging tendency to insult someone who's being kind to him

jokes.text: God,a scrapbook. Home run,michael. You set the bar so high. That was a bunt.
jokes.setup: Jim complimenting the thoughtful gift
jokes.punchline: Michael's baseball metaphor self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Michael's continued self-sabotage with sports metaphor

jokes.text: That was a bunt.
jokes.setup: Jim calling Michael's scrapbook a 'home run'
jokes.punchline: Michael's false modesty with baseball metaphor
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at modesty while still using sports metaphors to describe his romantic gesture

jokes.text: I want you to enjoy that cake because i have something terrible i need to tell you, and i want you to enjoy your cake before i tell you this terrible,terrible thing.
jokes.setup: Michael setting up to break up with Helene
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly emphasizing how terrible the news will be
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to just get to the point, making the buildup worse than necessary

jokes.text: Who is kafkaesque? I've never--i don't know him.
jokes.setup: Trying to sound intellectual
jokes.punchline: Thinking Kafkaesque is a person's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael intellectual pretension failing spectacularly

jokes.text: There's another woman,and her name is italy... and skydiving... And bungee jumping.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's breaking up with Helene
jokes.punchline: Personifying countries and activities as romantic rivals
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd way to explain wanting new experiences - treating activities as romantic competition

jokes.text: And you,unfortunately,have already completed that part of your journey down there.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining he wants kids
jokes.punchline: Horrifically inappropriate euphemism for menopause
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael cringe - discussing fertility in the most inappropriate way possible while breaking up

jokes.text: What if dwight dies and i still owe him something? That is a recipe for a ghost.
jokes.setup: Jim stuck owing Dwight a favor
jokes.punchline: Treating debt like it creates supernatural obligation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic delivered with Jim's perfect deadpan - treating favors like supernatural contracts

jokes.text: I could have grown poison mushrooms that'd be this high by now! They're mushrooms. They don't get that high.
jokes.setup: Dwight lamenting wasted time
jokes.punchline: Correcting his own exaggerated metaphor mid-rant
jokes.explanation: Dwight's dramatic exaggeration immediately undercut by his own literal accuracy about mushroom growth

jokes.text: Anthe string is attached from my heart to your mean attitude.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining 'strings attached' to raise offer
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical metaphor mixing strings with emotional connection
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at poetic bribery becomes confused metaphor about heartstrings

jokes.text: Talk to me,face.Tell me what pam's brain is thinking.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to read Pam's expression
jokes.punchline: Directly addressing her face as separate entity
jokes.explanation: Michael's desperate negotiation tactics escalating to talking to body parts

jokes.text: I want to hit you. What? I want to hit you.I'll do that.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what Pam wants
jokes.punchline: Pam's violent desire and Michael's immediate agreement
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from bribery to violence with Michael's spineless agreement

jokes.text: I'll take off my jacket and tense my stomach,and you just...
jokes.setup: Michael agreeing to let Pam hit him
jokes.punchline: Preparing for impact like a formal physical comedy routine
jokes.explanation: Michael's procedural approach to getting punched, treating it like a formal arrangement

jokes.text: And pam has that crazy pregnancy strength now.
jokes.setup: Warning Michael about the fight
jokes.punchline: Invoking mythical pregnancy superpowers
jokes.explanation: Funny concept of pregnancy giving supernatural strength

jokes.text: And pam has that crazy pregnancy strength now.
jokes.setup: Jim warning Michael about the upcoming punch
jokes.punchline: Treating pregnancy like it gives superhuman strength
jokes.explanation: Jim's protective husband concern mixed with absurd pregnancy mythology

jokes.text: Am i scared of getting hit in the face?No. Every day,weirdos pay dominatrices hundreds of dollars for that very privilege. I'm scared i'm gonna love it.
jokes.setup: Michael contemplating getting punched by Pam
jokes.punchline: Connecting workplace conflict to BDSM with disturbing self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny self-awareness from Michael, connecting his masochistic tendencies to the situation

jokes.text: For the record,your mom came on to me.
jokes.setup: Pam agreeing to forgive Michael after punching him
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately ruining the moment with defensive comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael timing - immediately destroying a peaceful resolution with unnecessary defensiveness

jokes.text: I saw my entire life flash before my eyes. And guess what. I have four kids. And i have a hover-car and a hover-house. And my wife is a runner,and it shows.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his life flashing before his eyes
jokes.punchline: Future fantasy mixing realistic goals with sci-fi and shallow concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael fantasy - mixing genuine desires with absurd future tech and shallow concerns about wife's body

jokes.text: Fire jim.
jokes.setup: Dwight cashing in his favor from Michael
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding Jim's firing after helping with ice
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to opening bagel plot, Dwight immediately using moment of vulnerability to strike

jokes.text: And this is my dinner on your face.I'm keeping it!
jokes.setup: Dwight's favor being rejected by Michael
jokes.punchline: Claiming ownership of ice/food on Michael's face
jokes.explanation: Dwight's petty revenge escalating to claiming ownership of ice on someone's face
